segments.title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: If you or someone you know has high blood pressure, this video will help you understand what it is and why it's important to keep it under control.

segments.title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: Over time, high blood pressure will damage the walls of your arteries. This damage can lead to life-threatening conditions.

segments.title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: For example, an artery wall may become weak and form an enlarged area called an aneurysm. The wall may burst and bleed into the tissue around it.

segments.title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: In another example, damage to an artery wall may attract certain substances in your blood, such as cholesterol, fat and calcium. They may form a buildup called a plaque.

segments.title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: In another example, damage to the arteries in your kidneys may reduce their ability to work properly. This can lead to kidney disease.

segments.title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: In a final example, damage to your arteries makes your heart beat even harder, which raises your blood pressure even more and can lead to heart failure.

segments.title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
segments.text_en: If you or someone you know has high blood pressure, this video will show you some ways to manage it.

segments.title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
segments.text_en: In most cases, the cause of high blood pressure or hypertension is unknown.

segments.title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
segments.text_en: This type of high blood pressure is called primary or essential hypertension.

segments.title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
segments.text_en: Treatment for it includes lifestyle changes such as eating a healthy diet.

segments.title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
segments.text_en: Losing weight if you need to.

segments.title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
segments.text_en: Your doctor may also recommend medications that act on your kidneys,

segments.title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
segments.text_en: blood vessels, or heart to help lower your blood pressure.

segments.title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
segments.text_en: Diuretics commonly called water pills cause your kidneys to move more salt and water from your blood into your urine.

segments.title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
segments.text_en: This reduces both the amount of your blood and your blood pressure.

segments.title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
segments.text_en: It is important to stay on your medications as directed even if you are feeling better.

segments.title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
segments.text_en: Do not go off your medication unless your doctor tells you to.

segments.title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
segments.text_en: If you have any questions about any medications you have been prescribed, speak with your doctor.

segments.text_en: High blood pressure is the main risk factor for any type of stroke.

segments.title: ANH18207 Cardiovascular Conditions and Type 2 Diabetes
segments.text_en: It is common for people with high blood pressure and abnormal lipid levels to also have type

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: People who have diabetes have a higher chance of developing many health problems, including

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: heart disease.

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: This video will show you how heart disease can develop, how it affects your health, and

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: what you can do to help reduce your risk of heart disease.

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: If you have diabetes, it means your blood glucose or blood sugar is elevated.

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: Over time, high blood sugar can damage the blood vessels of your heart and other organs,

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: leading to other health problems.

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: This means the longer you have diabetes, the greater your risk for heart disease.

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: Even both diabetes and heart disease can affect you in many ways.

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: It may be harder for you to go about your daily activities.

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: You may need help taking care of yourself.

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: You may also need to take more medications.

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: Living with these conditions may cause depression.

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: The good news is that there are things you can do to help lower your risk for heart disease.

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: For example, you may need to make changes to your lifestyle.

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: Both smoking and diabetes can narrow your blood vessels which can increase your chance of

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: developing heart disease or stroke.

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: Following the treatment plan you and your healthcare provider discussed will help you feel

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: better and reduce your risk of getting heart disease.

segments.text_en: tissues.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Every time you smoke a cigarette, toxic gases pass into your lungs, then into your blood

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: stream, where they spread to every organ in your body.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: A cigarette is made using the tobacco leaf, which contains nicotine and a variety of other

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: compounds. As the tobacco and compounds burn, they release thousands of dangerous chemicals,

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: including over 40 known to cause cancer.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Cigarette smoke contains the poisonous gases carbon monoxide and nitrogen oxide, as well

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: as trace amounts of cancer-causing radioactive particles.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: All forms of tobacco are dangerous, including cigars, pipes, and smokeless tobacco, such

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: as chewing tobacco and snuff.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Nicotine is an addictive chemical in tobacco. After you inhale tobacco smoke, nicotine flows

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: through the bloodstream to your brain, where it induces a pleasurable feeling.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: When you repeatedly expose your brain to nicotine, it becomes desensitized, making you crave

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: more and more nicotine just to feel normal.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Smoking causes death. People who smoke typically die at an earlier age than non-smokers.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: In fact, one of every five deaths in the United States is linked to cigarette smoking.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: If you smoke your risk of major health problems increases dramatically, including heart disease,

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: heart attack, stroke, lung cancer, and death from chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Smoking causes cardiovascular disease. When nicotine flows through your adrenal glands,

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: it stimulates the release of epinephrine, a hormone that raises your blood pressure.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: In addition, nicotine and carbon monoxide can damage the lining of the inner walls in

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: your arteries. Fatty deposits, called plaque, can build up at these injury sites and become

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: large enough to narrow the arteries and severely reduce blood flow, resulting in a condition

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: called atherosclerosis.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: In coronary artery disease, atherosclerosis narrows the arteries that supply the heart, which

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: reduces the supply of oxygen to your heart muscle, increasing your risk for a heart attack.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Smoking also raises your risk for blood clots, because it causes platelets in your blood

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: to clump together.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Smoking increases your risk for peripheral vascular disease, in which atherosclerotic

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: plaques block the large arteries in your arms and legs.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Smoking can also cause an abdominal aortic aneurysm, which is a swelling or weakening of

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: your aorta where it runs through your abdomen.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Smoking damages two main parts of your lungs.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Your airways also called bronchial tubes, and small air sacs called alveoli.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: With each breath, air travels down your windpipe, called the trachea, and enters your lungs

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: through your bronchial tubes.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Air then moves in two thousands of tiny alveoli, where oxygen from the air moves into your blood

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: stream, and the waste product carbon dioxide moves out of your blood stream.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Tiny hair-like projections, called cilia, line your bronchial tubes and sweep harmful substances

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: out of your lungs.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Cigarette smoke irritates the lining of your bronchial tubes, causing them to swell and make

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: mucus.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Cigarette smoke also slows the movement of your cilia, causing some of the smoke and

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: mucus to stay in your lungs.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: While you're sleeping, some of the cilia recover and start pushing more pollutants and

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: mucus out of your lungs.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: When you wake up, your body attempts to expel this material by coughing repeatedly, a

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: condition known as smokers cough.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Over time, chronic bronchitis develops as your cilia stop working, your airways become

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: clogged with scars and mucus, and breathing becomes difficult.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Your lungs are now more vulnerable to further disease.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Cigarette smoke also damages your LVLI, making it harder for oxygen and carbon dioxide

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: to exchange with your blood.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Over time, so little oxygen can reach your blood that you may develop emphysema, a condition

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: in which you must gasp for every breath and wear an oxygen tube under your nose in order

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: to breathe.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Chronic bronchitis and emphysema are collectively called chronic obstructive pulmonary disease

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: or COPD.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: COPD is a gradual loss of the ability to breathe for which there is no cure.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Cigarette smoke contains at least 40 cancer-causing substances called carcinogens, including cyanide,

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: formaldehyde, benzene, and ammonia.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: In your body, healthy cells grow, make new cells, then die.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Genetic material inside each cell, called DNA, directs this process.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: If you smoke toxic chemicals, can damage the DNA in your healthy cells.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: As a result, your damaged cells create new unhealthy cells, which grow out of control

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: and may spread to other parts of your body.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: The most common cancer in the world is lung cancer, with over a million new cases diagnosed

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: every year.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Harmful chemicals in cigarettes can cause cancer in other parts of your body, such as, in the

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: blood and bone marrow, mouth, larynx, throat, esophagus, stomach, pancreas, kidney, bladder,

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: uterus, and cervix.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Cigarette can cause infertility in both men and women.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: If a woman is pregnant and smokes during pregnancy, she exposes her baby to the cigarette's

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: poisonous chemicals, causing a greater risk of low birth weight, miscarriage, pre-term delivery,

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: still birth, infant death, and sudden infant death syndrome.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Hitting is also dangerous if a mother is breastfeeding.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Nicotine passes to the baby through breast milk, and can cause restlessness, rapid heartbeat,

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: vomiting, interrupted sleep, or diarrhea.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Other health effects of smoking include low bone density and increased risk for hip fracture

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: among women, gum disease often leading to tooth loss and surgery, immune system dysfunction,

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: delayed wound healing, and sexual impotence in men.

segments.title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
segments.text_en: Smoking, lack of physical activity, an unhealthy diet, and stress.

segments.title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
segments.text_en: And quitting smoking.

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: If you smoke, get the help you need to quit.

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: Both smoking and diabetes can narrow your blood vessels which can increase your chance of

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: developing heart disease or stroke.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: If you smoke, quitting is one of the most important things you can do for your heart.

segments.text_en: When the blood supply is not able to keep up with the demand, the heart muscle cells don't

segments.text_en: Be mindful of any symptoms of chest pain, pressure, tightness, heaviness, ache, or any other

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4

segments.text_en: If you have acute coronary syndrome, blood flow through your coronary arteries is severely

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: reduced or completely blocked.

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4

segments.text_en: In this condition, a build-up of a fatty substance called plaque can narrow your coronary

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: arteries.

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: Other less common causes of reduced blood flow include coronary artery spasm or dissection.

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: In a coronary artery spasm, triggers such as drugs, smoking, cold weather, and extreme

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: stress or emotions can cause a temporary and sudden tightening of a coronary artery.

segments.text_en: During a coronary artery dissection, the inside wall of one of your coronary arteries separates

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: which can block blood flow.

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: Regardless of the cause, a blockage in either coronary artery prevents the oxygen and nutrients

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: in your blood from reaching the part of your heart supplied by the artery.

segments.text_en: A blocked coronary artery may also cause you to feel sudden pain, discomfort, tightening,

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: This video will help you understand a heart attack and how to manage it.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Please watch the entire video to learn how to manage your recovery.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: If you have been hospitalized with a heart attack, it's important to recover properly when

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: you return home.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: A heart attack, also called a myocardial infarction, or MI, occurs when blood flow to an area of

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: your heart is greatly reduced.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: When the blood supply is not able to keep up with the demand, the heart muscle cells don't

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: get enough blood to stay alive.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: The result is death of heart muscle cells in the affected area of the heart.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: The goals after a heart attack are to keep your heart healthy and avoid another heart attack.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Recovery takes time and the amount is different for each patient.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: It depends upon how much of your heart muscle is damaged and your individual rate of recovery.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: You may also have had a procedure.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: This may also affect your recovery time.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: You can lower your risk of having another heart attack by taking these steps, follow your

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: doctor's instructions, make some lifestyle changes, and know when to seek medical attention.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Following the instructions of your doctor and healthcare team is very important to your recovery.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: This includes taking your medications exactly as prescribed by your doctor.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Don't stop taking your medications or skip doses.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Take note of any side effects and tell your doctor.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Don't take any over-the-counter medication or supplement without first consulting your doctor.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Tell your doctor about any other prescriptions you're taking.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Some medications can prevent your heart medicine from working.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: It's important that your recovery is monitored.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Keep all of your follow-up appointments.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Have exams done as directed by your doctor.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: For example, if your doctor has told you to check your blood pressure regularly, do

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: as directed.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Lowering your risk of another heart attack usually requires some lifestyle changes.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Avoid a heart-healthy diet, including low fat, low cholesterol, and high fiber foods.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: A heart-healthy diet helps you maintain or achieve a good blood pressure and weight.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: It also helps prevent other conditions that may further stress your heart.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Maintain a healthy weight.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Allow yourself time to heal by taking it easy for the first four weeks.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Getting a good night's sleep.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Rest in the afternoon.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Avoid heavy lifting and gradually increasing your physical activity.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Ask your doctor when you can resume driving, sexual activity, and return to work.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: For weeks after your heart attack, you will begin cardiac rehab, which includes a closely

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: monitored exercise regimen.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: It is important to stay aware of changes in your symptoms and know when to get help.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: If your hospital stay included a procedure with an incision, check the site each day for

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: any signs of infection.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Call your doctor if you develop a fever or redness, heat, pain, tenderness, pus, or bleeding

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: at the incision site.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Be mindful of any symptoms of chest pain, pressure, tightness, heaviness, ache, or any other

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: kind of unusual sensation in your chest as any of these could mean you have a blockage

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Call 911 immediately if you have any of these symptoms.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Angina that doesn't go away with medication is prescribed by your doctor.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Shortness of breath.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Cold sweats.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: nausea.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Vomiting.

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Or lightheadedness.

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: If you have acute coronary syndrome, you have one or more conditions caused by a blockage

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: of blood flow to your heart muscle.

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: This is a medical emergency because you may be having a heart attack, a condition in which

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: your heart muscle begins to die.

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: Regardless of the cause, a blockage in either coronary artery prevents the oxygen and nutrients

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: in your blood from reaching the part of your heart supplied by the artery.

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: As a result, heart muscle in that area starts to die.

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: Death of part of your heart muscle is called a heart attack.

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: It's also known as a myocardial infarction or MI.

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: Other symptoms of acute coronary syndrome include shortness of breath, dizziness, nausea,

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: and sweating.

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: If you've had a heart attack or have other types of acute coronary syndrome, your doctor

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: may prescribe oxygen therapy to get more oxygen into your blood.

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: You may also receive drugs called beta blockers that slow down your heart and reduce its need

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: for oxygen.

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: Seek treatment immediately if you have the symptoms of acute coronary syndrome.

segments.text_en: Using your heart attack, blood flow through one of your coronary arteries may have been

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: If this plaque became disrupted, a blood clot might form and severely worsen the narrowing

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: or lead to a sudden complete blockage stopping blood flow down the artery.

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: A blockage in your coronary arteries prevented the oxygen and nutrients in your blood from

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: reaching the part of your heart supplied by the artery.

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: As a result, heart muscle in that area started to die.

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: Damage to part of your heart muscle is called a heart attack.

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: It's also known as a myocardial infarction, or MI.

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: Before you left the hospital, your healthcare provider most likely prescribed several medications.

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack

segments.text_en: attack.

segments.text_en: and even death.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: The most serious aridhmia is ventricular fibrillation, where many random impulses fire rapidly within

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: the ventricular walls.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: In ventricular fibrillation, the ventricles are quivering instead of beating.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: This is a medical emergency because the heart cannot effectively pump blood to the body

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: or itself.

segments.title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
segments.text_en: Your doctor may want you to have an implantable cardio-verter defibrillator or ICD to restore

segments.title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
segments.text_en: the heart's normal rhythm and prevent sudden cardiac death.

segments.text_en: Symptoms related to blockage in the blood vessels of the heart are called angina and could

segments.file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
segments.title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
segments.text_en: Angina symptoms may also be felt in your arms, shoulders, neck, jaw, throat, or back.

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
segments.title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
segments.text_en: or a burning sensation in your chest called angina.

segments.file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4

segments.text_en: This pain may extend to your upper abdomen, shoulders, arms, neck, and lower jaw.

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: A surgeon will insert a pacemaker into your body if your heart's conduction system,

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: the electrical system that makes your heart beat, is not working properly.

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: The heart has a natural pacemaker called the Sinoatrial node or SA node.

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: The pacemaker controls the heart rate by precisely determining when the muscles of the atria

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: and ventricles contract.

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: The electrical signal produced by the SA node reaches the ventricles through a narrow pathway

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: that includes the so-called AV node and bundle branches.

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: Taken together, these pathways are referred to as the conduction system of the heart.

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: Problems with the conduction system can result if the SA node malfunctions, causing the

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: The heart normally beats in a regular rhythm at 60 to 100 beats per minute.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: This steady and coordinated rhythm allows the heart to effectively pump blood.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: The electrical signals that travel through the heart control the heart's rhythm.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: These electrical signals start in a cluster of cells called the Sinoatrial node or pacemaker.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: The Sinoatrial node or SA node is located in the wall of the right upper chamber of the

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: heart, called the right atrium.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: When the electrical signals reach the heart muscle, they cause it to contract and pump blood.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: If needed, an electric shock will be given to restart the heart.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Generally, temporary pacemaker wires will be placed on the surface of the heart to ensure the heart maintains a normal rhythm during recovery.

segments.title: ANH11047 Atrial Fibrillation
segments.text_en: Atrial fibrillation is a type of arrhythmia, which is an abnormality in the pace or force

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: When special cells, called pacemaker cells, generate electrical signals inside your heart.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: The heart muscle cells, called myocytes, contract as a group.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: Depending on activity level, the heart beats about 60 to 100 times per minute.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: It may be higher during exercise or lower at rest.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: A normal heart rate and rhythm ensures the delivery of oxygen-rich blood to all of the

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: body's organs, such as the brain and lungs.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: A group of cells in the heart, called the cardiac conduction system, uses electrical

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: impulses to control the speed and rhythm of each heartbeat.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: Each heartbeat starts in the right atrium in the sinusoidal atrial or SA node, then spreads

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: through the walls of the heart chambers, called the atria, and ventricles, causing them

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: to contract.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: This process repeats with each heartbeat.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: Depending on activity level, the heart beats about 60 to 100 times per minute.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: It may be higher during exercise or lower at rest.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: A normal heart rate and rhythm ensures the delivery of oxygen-rich blood to all of the

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: body's organs, such as the brain and lungs.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: A group of cells in the heart, called the cardiac conduction system, uses electrical

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: impulses to control the speed and rhythm of each heartbeat.

segments.title: ANS00254 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed
segments.text_en: With the grafts securely in place, your surgeon will use electrical signals to restore the

segments.title: ANS00254 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed
segments.text_en: heart beat and attach a temporary pacemaker to the heart.

segments.title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: If needed, an electric shock will be given to restart the heart.

segments.title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: Generally, temporary pacemaker wires will be placed on the surface of the heart to ensure

segments.title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: the heart maintains a normal rhythm during recovery.

segments.title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
segments.text_en: Rhythmic electrical impulses inside your heart cause its muscular walls to contract, ensuring

segments.title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
segments.text_en: your heart beats steadily.

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: A surgeon will insert a pacemaker into your body if your heart's conduction system,

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: the electrical system that makes your heart beat, is not working properly.

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: Problems with the conduction system can result if the SA node malfunctions, causing the

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: heart to beat too fast, too slow or irregularly.

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: Alternatively, a blockage in the bundle branches can develop, causing the heart to beat more

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: slowly or irregularly.

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: These problems can be due to heart disease, medications, or cardiac surgery.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: If you have an abnormal heart rhythm, your doctor may perform electrical cardioversion,

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: where he or she uses an electrical shock to restore your normal heart beat.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: An abnormal heart rhythm is called an arrhythmia.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: During atrial fibrillation, the atria quiver because of chaotic electrical signals in the

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: heart, resulting in an abnormally fast or irregular heartbeat.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: Because of the abnormal heartbeat, blood may pool in the atria, which can result in

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: the formation of a blood clot.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: If the blood clot passes into the general circulation, it may cause a stroke.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: Before the procedure, you will be connected to an intravenous line.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: You will be sedated so you will sleep through the procedure.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: EKG electrodes will be placed on your chest to monitor your heart's electrical activity.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: Once you are sedated, special cardioversion pads or paddles will be placed on the skin

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: of your chest or your chest and back.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: These pads or paddles will deliver electrical shock through your chest to your heart.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: The shock may be repeated several times until the heart resumes its normal rhythm.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: After your electrical cardioversion, you will be monitored closely in a recovery room

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: or in the coronary care unit of the hospital.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: Once the sedation wears off and you are in stable condition, you will be set home.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Generally, temporary pacemaker wires will be placed on the surface of the heart to ensure the heart maintains a normal rhythm during recovery.

segments.text_en: Atrial fibrillation is a type of arrhythmia, which is an abnormality in the pace or force

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: muscle, irregular heartbeats called arrhythmias, heart defects, poisons or substance abuse,

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: Problems with the cardiac conduction system cause the heart to have an abnormal rhythm

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: called aniridhmia.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: This may cause an irregular pulse.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: Aridhmia may happen in the atria or ventricles.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: Ritms of aridmias include fibrillation, which is an irregular heartbeat rhythm, tachycardia,

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: which is a fast heartbeat of more than 100 beats per minute, and bradycardia, which is a

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: slow heartbeat of less than 60 beats per minute.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: Fibrillation is the most common type of aridhmia.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: Random impulses cause the atria to fibrillate or twitch rapidly and randomly.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: Tachycardia in the atria is called supraventricular tachycardia.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: In focal atrial tachycardia, small areas within the atria wall start or pass along impulses

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: that cause the atria to contract rapidly, but with irregular rhythm.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: In atrial flutter, larger areas within the atrial wall start or pass along impulses

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: that cause the atria to contract rapidly, but with irregular rhythm.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: Tachycardia may also happen in the ventricles with rapid and regular contractions.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: The body may not receive enough blood because the ventricles contract before completely

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: filling with blood.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: The most serious aridhmia is ventricular fibrillation, where many random impulses fire rapidly within

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: the ventricular walls.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: In ventricular fibrillation, the ventricles are quivering instead of beating.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: This is a medical emergency because the heart cannot effectively pump blood to the body

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: or itself.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: Sometimes problems with the SA node or problems with the pathway of the electrical impulses

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: to the ventricles can cause the slow heartbeat in bradycardia.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: If the heartbeat is too slowly, the body may not receive enough oxygen-rich blood.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: Depending on the type of aridhmia, a doctor may recommend one or more of the following

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: treatments.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: Life style changes, such as eating a heart-healthy diet, exercising, and quitting smoking.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: Medication, such as anti-aridmic drugs and beta blockers, catheter ablation, where thin

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: wires inserted into the heart, destroy the tissue causing the aridhmia with hot or cold

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: energy, and implantable devices such as a pacemaker or defibrillator to correct the pace

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: or rhythm of the heart.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: An abnormal heart rate or rhythm, called an arrhythmia, occurs when there's a problem

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: with the heart's conduction system.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: Taca-cardia is a type of arrhythmia where the heart beats too fast.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: Fibrillation is a type of arrhythmia where the heart beats irregularly and may be too

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: fast.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: For certain types of arrhythmias, a catheter ablation procedure may be necessary to stop the

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: heart tissue from causing the arrhythmia.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: After numbing a small area in the groin with a needle, the doctor will insert a short

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: hollow tube called a catheter sheath into the femoral vein.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: Next, a long flexible tube called a catheter will be inserted through the sheath.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: The doctor will guide the catheter to the heart through a blood vessel that goes to the

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: heart, called the inferior vena-cava.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: The location and progress of the catheter will be monitored.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: When the catheter reaches the heart, the doctor will guide it to the area that is causing

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: the arrhythmia.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: The doctor will find the problem area is using a 3D map of the electrical activity of

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: the patient's heart.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: The tip of the catheter will emit either hot energy or cold energy to ablate the tissue

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: in this area.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: The patient makes the treated area stop working.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: For an atrial arrhythmia, a doctor will ablate the atrial tissue causing it.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: If the affected tissues are small, well-defined areas, the procedure is called focal ablation.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: Or if the affected tissues are larger areas with more complex rhythm disturbances, the doctor

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: may perform a procedure called ablation remodeling.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: Both types of ablation restore normal electrical impulses and prevent an arrhythmia from happening.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: If the cause of the arrhythmia is in the ventricle, the doctor can do either focal ablation

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: or ablation remodeling to treat more complex arrhythmias of the ventricle.

segments.title: ANS11476 Atrial Fibrillation (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: Atrial fibrillation is a type of arrhythmia, which is an abnormality in the pace or force

segments.title: ANS11476 Atrial Fibrillation (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: of your heartbeat.

segments.title: ANS11476 Atrial Fibrillation (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: If you have atrial fibrillation, the heart's upper chambers contract in a fast, twitchy

segments.title: ANS11476 Atrial Fibrillation (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: fashion, resulting in an irregular, uncoordinated heartbeat.

segments.title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
segments.text_en: If problems occur with your heart's electrical system, you may develop an abnormal, potentially

segments.title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
segments.text_en: life-threatening heart beat called arrhythmia.

segments.title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
segments.text_en: Your doctor may want you to have an implantable cardio-verter defibrillator or ICD to restore

segments.title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
segments.text_en: the heart's normal rhythm and prevent sudden cardiac death.

segments.title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
segments.text_en: During the procedure, your doctor will thread a small wire through a vein until it reaches

segments.title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
segments.text_en: your right ventricle.

segments.title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
segments.text_en: The wires continuously monitor the heart's rhythm.

segments.title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
segments.text_en: After verifying the wire is in the correct location and works properly, your doctor will

segments.title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
segments.text_en: attach the wire to the ICD generator.

segments.title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
segments.text_en: Then your doctor will insert the ICD device under the skin of your chest below the collarbone.

segments.title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
segments.text_en: The battery-powered ICD constantly monitors your heart beat using a computer.

segments.title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
segments.text_en: If a life-threatening arrhythmia occurs, the ICD delivers an electrical shock to your

segments.title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
segments.text_en: heart, resetting your heart beat back to normal.

segments.title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
segments.text_en: The ICD can also be programmed to paste the heart and restore its natural rhythm.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: To make up for the decreased amount of blood, your nervous system releases stress hormones

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: that increase the speed and force of your heart beat.

segments.title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
segments.text_en: Beta blockers reduce the workload on your heart in two ways.

segments.title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
segments.text_en: They slow your heart beat and lower the force of each beat.

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: You may have also received drugs called beta blockers that help lower your heart rate

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: and blood pressure.

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: To make up for the decreased amount of blood, your nervous system releases stress hormones

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: that increase the speed and force of your heart beat.

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: Unfortunately, the continued release of these stress hormones makes your heart fail

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: your worse because they damage the muscle cells in your ventricles.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Medication
segments.text_en: Beta blockers are medications that block the effects of stress hormones on your heart.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Medication
segments.text_en: Although beta blockers slow down your heartbeat, they are mainly used to protect your heart muscle from the long-term damage caused by stress hormones.

segments.title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
segments.text_en: If you have been diagnosed with heart failure, it's important to control your symptoms.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: After a period of time, a faulty aortic valve can cause the heart muscles to become enlarged and weakened.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Depending on the nature of the valve's defect, the walls of the left ventricle may become abnormally thick or thin,

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: and the problem can eventually affect the right side of the heart as well.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Patients with this condition known as heart failure may experience shortness of breath, chest pain or angina pectoris,

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: dizziness, lightheadedness, and or fainting, swelling of the ankles, feet and legs, and fatigue.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: If you have heart failure, your heart has lost the ability to pump enough blood to meet

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: your body's needs.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: You may have weak or damaged ventricular walls that are not able to push enough blood

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: out of your heart.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: You may also have stiff and thickened ventricular walls that do not allow your heart to fill

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: with enough blood.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: If you have left-sided heart failure, your left ventricle does not deliver enough oxygen-rich

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: blood to your body, making you feel tired and out of breath.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: Your failing left ventricle also increases the blood pressure in the blood vessels between

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: your lungs and left ventricle.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: This increased pressure forces fluid out of your blood and into your lung tissues, which

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: makes it difficult for you to breathe.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: If you have right-sided heart failure, your right ventricle is unable to contract with

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: enough force to push blood to your lungs.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: The result is a build-up of blood in your veins, which causes a build-up of fluid called

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: edema throughout the tissues in your body.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: Over time, heart failure on either side of your heart results in weakened and large

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: ventricles that deliver less blood to your body.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: To make up for the decreased amount of blood, your nervous system releases stress hormones

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: that increase the speed and force of your heart beat.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: Unfortunately, the continued release of these stress hormones makes your heart failure

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: worse because they damage the muscle cells in your ventricles.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: The most common cause of heart failure is coronary artery disease.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: Other causes that damage your heart and lead to heart failure include high blood pressure,

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: diabetes, diseased infected or damaged heart valves, diseased infected or damaged heart

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: muscle, irregular heartbeats called arrhythmias, heart defects, poisons or substance abuse,

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: lung diseases, and breathing problems during sleep called sleep apnea.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: Treatment for heart failure includes medications, lifestyle changes, or in extreme heart failure,

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: heart beat assisting devices or surgery.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: Medications reduce the swelling in your body by increasing the amount of urine produced

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: by your kidneys.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: Ace inhibitors are medications that allow your blood vessels to expand.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: This helps decrease your blood pressure and prevents further damage to your heart by making

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: it easier for your heart to pump blood.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: Beta blockers are medications that block the effects of stress hormones on your heart.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: Although beta blockers slow down your heart beat, they are mainly used to protect your

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: heart muscle from the long-term damage caused by stress hormones.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: You may need to make some lifestyle changes, including exercising on a regular basis,

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: maintaining a healthy weight, quitting smoking, limiting salt and alcohol,

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: and eating a heart-healthy diet.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: Surgery for advanced heart failure include coronary artery bypass surgery to improve blood flow to your heart muscle,

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: heart valve reconstruction surgery, to improve blood flow through your heart,

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: and left ventricle reconstruction surgery to remove damaged heart muscle.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: Surgeries for extreme heart failure include insertion of a device to help your heart pump blood,

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: and heart transplant.

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: When left-sided heart failure, the left ventricle is not able to pump enough blood to the body.

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: The two types of left-handed heart failure are diastolic, where the left ventricle can't

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: relax enough to fill with blood, and more commonly systolic, where the left ventricle

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: can't contract strongly enough to push blood to the body.

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: In severe or end-stage heart failure, the heart is getting weaker and doesn't respond

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: to medication.

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: For left-sided end-stage systolic heart failure, a doctor may recommend an implantable left

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: ventricular assist device, or LVAD, to help the heart pump enough blood to the body.

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: An LVAD is a small mechanical pump implanted inside the chest or abdomen that takes over

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: the job of the weak left ventricle.

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: Blood flows from the left ventricle through an inflow tube to the pump.

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: The pump pushes the blood through an outflow tube into the aorta.

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: From here, the blood travels normally from the aorta to the rest of the body.

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: A cable, called a drive line, connects the LVAD pump inside the chest to a control unit

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: located on the outside of the body.

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: The control unit is a small computer that controls the functions of the pump and alerts

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: the patient if there are any problems.

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: The pump and control unit receive power through a cord connected to a battery pack.

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: Finally, the control unit and battery pack are held in a lightweight patient pack.

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: An LVAD may be a temporary treatment until a heart transplant can be performed, or if

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: the patient isn't able to have a heart transplant, an LVAD may be used for destination therapy,

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: which is long-term treatment.

segments.title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: In a final example, damage to your arteries makes your heart beat even harder, which raises your blood pressure even more and can lead to heart failure.

segments.title: ANS00426 The Aortic Valve
