segments.title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
segments.text_en: Any condition or disease that damages your heart's ability to pump blood can cause

segments.title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
segments.text_en: heart failure.

segments.title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
segments.text_en: For example, the most common cause is coronary artery disease in which blood vessels that

segments.title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
segments.text_en: supply your heart muscle, called coronary arteries, become blocked by a fatty substance called

segments.title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
segments.text_en: plaque.

segments.title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
segments.text_en: Other common causes of heart failure include uncontrolled high blood pressure and high

segments.title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
segments.text_en: blood sugar in diabetes.

segments.title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
segments.text_en: Less common causes of heart failure include diseased, infected or damaged heart valves,

segments.title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
segments.text_en: your regular heartbeats, called arrhythmias, heart defects, poisons or substance abuse, lung

segments.title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
segments.text_en: diseases, and breathing problems during sleep, called sleep apnea.

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: If you have heart failure, your heart has lost the ability to pump enough blood to meet

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: your body's needs.

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: You may have weak or damaged ventricular walls that are not able to push enough blood

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: out of your heart.

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: You may also have stiff and thickened ventricular walls that do not allow your heart to fill

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: with enough blood.

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: If you have left-sided heart failure, your left ventricle does not deliver enough oxygen

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: rich blood to your body, making you feel tired and out of breath.

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: Your failing left ventricle also increases the blood pressure in the blood vessels between

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: your lungs and left ventricle.

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: This increased pressure forces fluid out of your blood and into your lung tissues, which

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: makes it difficult for you to breathe.

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: If you have right-sided heart failure, your right ventricle is unable to contract with

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: enough force to push blood to your lungs.

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: The result is a build-up of blood in your veins, which causes a build-up of fluid called

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: edema throughout the tissues in your body.

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: Over time, heart failure on either side of your heart results in weakened and large

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: ventricles that deliver less blood to your body.

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: To make up for the decreased amount of blood, your nervous system releases stress hormones

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: that increase the speed and force of your heart beat.

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: Unfortunately, the continued release of these stress hormones makes your heart fail

segments.title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
segments.text_en: your worse because they damage the muscle cells in your ventricles.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: This video will help you understand some lifestyle changes you can make to treat heart failure.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: Please watch the entire video to learn about lifestyle changes for heart failure.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: As a heart failure patient, you may need to make some lifestyle changes to prevent further damage to your heart.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: If you smoke, quitting is one of the most important things you can do for your heart.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: Work with your healthcare provider to create a nutritious eating plan that also helps you maintain a healthy weight.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: A heart healthy diet includes vegetables, fruits, lean meats and whole grains, as well as low fat or fat-free dairy products.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: High calorie foods such as cookies and soda should be replaced with fruits and vegetables that don't contain added salt and sugar.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: Examples of foods in a healthy diet include vegetables such as carrots, broccoli and tomatoes, fruits such as apples, oranges and strawberries,

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: lean proteins such as fish, chicken and beans,

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: dairy foods such as low fat yogurt and cheese,

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: starches such as whole wheat bread and sweet potatoes,

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: and healthy drinks such as water and fat-free milk.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: Reduce the amount of salt and sugar you eat.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: Keep sodium levels below 2000mg to reduce the amount of water retained by your body.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: Substitute solid fats with heart healthy oils such as olive oil.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: Limit the amount of fluid you drink.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: Ask your healthcare provider how much fluid you should drink each day.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: And remember, a heart healthy diet doesn't include alcohol.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: Also, check with your healthcare provider about an exercise plan that's right for you.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: 30 minutes of daily low impact exercise can help strengthen your heart.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
segments.text_en: Talk to your healthcare provider if you have any questions about lifestyle changes to help you manage heart failure.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Medication
segments.text_en: Your doctor may prescribe one or more types of medication to treat your heart failure.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Medication
segments.text_en: Diuretic medications reduce the swelling in your body by increasing the amount of urine produced by your kidneys.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Medication
segments.text_en: Ace inhibitors are medications that allow your blood vessels to expand.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Medication
segments.text_en: This helps decrease your blood pressure and prevents further damage to your heart by making it easier for your heart to pump blood.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Medication
segments.text_en: Beta blockers are medications that block the effects of stress hormones on your heart.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Medication
segments.text_en: Although beta blockers slow down your heartbeat, they are mainly used to protect your heart muscle from the long-term damage caused by stress hormones.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
segments.text_en: This video will help you understand the main surgical procedures done to treat heart failure.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
segments.text_en: Please watch the entire video to learn about the surgeries for heart failure.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
segments.text_en: Surgery for advanced heart failure include coronary artery bypass surgery to improve blood flow to your heart muscle.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
segments.text_en: And heart valve reconstruction surgery to improve blood flow through your heart.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
segments.text_en: If the heart remains weak with standard heart failure treatments,

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
segments.text_en: advanced treatment options for severe heart failure include insertion of a pump for your heart.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
segments.text_en: A ventricular assist device, or VAD, is a mechanical pump that's used to support heart function and blood flow in people who have weakened hearts.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
segments.text_en: The device takes blood from a lower chamber of the heart and helps pump it to the body and vital organs just as a healthy heart would.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
segments.text_en: Another advanced treatment for severe heart failure is a heart transplant.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
segments.text_en: If you have advanced heart failure, your surgeon will determine the best type of treatment for you.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
segments.text_en: The first step is to get the heart transplant.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
segments.text_en: The second step is to get the heart transplant.

segments.title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
segments.text_en: The second step is to get the heart transplant.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: High blood pressure or hypertension is a common condition in which the force of blood on

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: the walls of your arteries is often too high.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: Arteries are the blood vessels that carry blood away from your heart to supply your tissues

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: with oxygen and nutrients.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: As blood flows through them, three main factors affect the pressure on your artery walls.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: The first is cardiac output or the amount of blood your ventricles push out of your

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: heart each minute.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: Your blood pressure goes up as cardiac output increases.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: The second factor affecting your blood pressure is blood volume or the total amount of blood

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: in your body.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: Blood pressure also goes up as blood volume increases.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: The third factor that affects your blood pressure is resistance, which is anything working

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: against the blood flow through your arteries.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: Several factors contribute to resistance.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: Healthy arteries expand with each heartbeat to help reduce blood pressure on the wall.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: Another resistance factor is the diameter of your arteries.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: Your body is able to increase the diameter of your arteries to lower your blood pressure

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: or reduce the diameter to raise your blood pressure.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: A third resistance factor is blood viscosity or thickness.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: In your blood, more particles, such as proteins and fat, increase viscosity.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: If your blood is thicker, your blood pressure goes up as your heart works harder to push

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: it through your arteries.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: When your heart beats, the pressure of blood on the walls of your arteries is called systolic

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: pressure.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: When your heart relaxes between beats, pressure on the artery wall is called diastolic pressure.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: While your blood pressure may change throughout the day, it should optimally be less than 120

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: millimeters of mercury for systolic pressure and less than 80 millimeters of mercury for

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: diastolic pressure.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: If your systolic pressure frequently stays above 130 or your diastolic pressure frequently

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: stays above 80, you have high blood pressure.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: In most cases, the cause of high blood pressure or hypertension is unknown.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: This type of high blood pressure is called primary or essential hypertension.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: Treatment for essential hypertension includes lifestyle changes, such as eating a healthy

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: diet.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: Other lifestyle changes that can reduce blood pressure include avoiding excessive alcohol

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: intake, getting regular exercise, losing weight if you are overweight, and quitting smoking,

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: and your doctor may also recommend medications that act on your kidneys, blood vessels,

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: or heart to help reduce your blood pressure.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: Diuretics commonly called water pills cause your kidneys to move more salt and water

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: from your blood into your urine, which reduces your blood volume and pressure.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: Beta blockers reduce the workload on your heart by decreasing both the rate of your heart

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: beat and the strength of your heart's contractions.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: Several types of drugs act directly or indirectly to reduce your blood pressure by relaxing your

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: blood vessels, which increases their diameter.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: These drugs include ACE inhibitors, angiotensin-2 receptor blockers, calcium channel blockers,

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: and direct acting vasodilators.

segments.text_en: This video will help you understand what the numbers in your blood pressure mean.

segments.file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
segments.title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
segments.text_en: When your heart beats, the pressure of blood on the walls of your arteries is called systolic pressure.

segments.file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
segments.title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
segments.text_en: When your heart relaxes between beats, pressure on the artery wall is called diastolic pressure.

segments.file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
segments.title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
segments.text_en: Your blood pressure may change throughout the day.

segments.file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
segments.title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
segments.text_en: However, optimal systolic pressure should be less than 120 and optimal diastolic pressure should be less than 80.

segments.file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
segments.title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
segments.text_en: If your systolic pressure frequently stays above 130 or your diastolic pressure frequently stays above 80, you have high blood pressure.

segments.file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
segments.title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
segments.text_en: Your specific goals may vary depending on your health situation.

segments.file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
segments.title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
segments.text_en: Ask your doctor what your blood pressure goals should be.

segments.title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
segments.text_en: This video will help you understand how blood pressure is controlled in your body.

segments.title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
segments.text_en: Blood pressure is the amount of force caused by blood pressing against the walls of your arteries.

segments.title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
segments.text_en: It is tightly controlled to ensure sufficient blood reaches all parts of your body.

segments.title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
segments.text_en: As blood flows through your arteries four main factors affect the pressure on the artery walls.

segments.title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
segments.text_en: The first is the amount of blood your ventricles push out of your heart each minute, also known as cardiac output.

segments.title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
segments.text_en: Your blood pressure goes up as cardiac output increases.

segments.title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
segments.text_en: The second factor affecting your blood pressure is the total amount of blood in your body or blood volume.

segments.title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
segments.text_en: Blood pressure also goes up as blood volume increases.

segments.title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
segments.text_en: The third factor that affects your blood pressure is resistance.

segments.title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
segments.text_en: Resistance is anything working against the blood flow through your arteries.

segments.title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
segments.text_en: Resistance is mainly affected by the width of your arteries.

segments.title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
segments.text_en: Your body is able to increase the width of your arteries to lower your blood pressure.

segments.title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
segments.text_en: Or it can reduce the width to raise your blood pressure.

segments.title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
segments.text_en: The fourth factor that affects your blood pressure is viscosity.

segments.title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
segments.text_en: Viscosity is how thick your blood is.

segments.title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
segments.text_en: In your blood more particles such as proteins and fat increase viscosity.

segments.title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
segments.text_en: If your blood is thicker your blood pressure goes up as your heart works harder to push it through your arteries.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: A coronary angioplasty procedure is also known as percutaneous coronary intervention.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: The procedure is done on blood vessels called coronary arteries.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: They supply your heart muscle with oxygen.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: The goal is to restore blood flow if a substance called plaque has significantly narrowed

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: these vessels.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: To begin the procedure, the doctor will numb the skin in your wrist.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: A needle will be placed through your skin and into your radial artery.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: Next, a flexible guide wire will be passed through the needle into your artery.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: Then, the needle will be withdrawn.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: It will be exchanged for a small, flexible tube called a sheath.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: This permits access into your artery.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: You may feel pressure when the doctor inserts the sheath, but you will not feel it moving

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: inside your artery.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: Next, the guide wire will be advanced up to your heart.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: A flexible tube called a catheter will be advanced over the wire to your coronary arteries.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: The progress of the procedure will be checked with an x-ray device called a flora scope.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: At this point, your doctor will remove the guide wire.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: Then, the doctor will move the tip of the catheter just inside the coronary artery to be examined.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: A special die will be injected into the artery.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: This allows your doctor to view it better with a flora scope.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: The die will make any blockages in the artery stand out.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: If a significant blockage is found, your doctor will insert a guide wire into the artery.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: A balloon on the tip of the catheter will be moved along the wire to the blockage.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: When the balloon inflates, it will expand the artery and improve the blood flow.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: You may feel some chest discomfort while this is happening.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: After this, your doctor will deflate and remove the balloon.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: A wire mesh tube called a stent may be placed in the treated area.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: The stent helps keep the coronary artery open.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: Your doctor will choose the proper size stent, which is compressed over a balloon.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: The stent will be moved into the artery over the same guide wire.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: When the balloon is inflated, the stent will expand and lock into place.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: After the balloon catheter is taken out, the stent will stay in place to hold the artery open.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: At the end of the procedure, the guide wire will be removed.

segments.title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
segments.text_en: To find out more about coronary artery angioplasty, talk to your healthcare provider.

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: Your heart doctor may have recommended a procedure to help open the blockage and improve

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: blood flow to the damaged area.

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: The procedure you had may have been a coronary angioplasty.

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: During a coronary angioplasty, a balloon-tipped catheter inflates inside your blocked coronary

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: artery to open it.

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: The procedure may have involved placing a stent to help prop the artery open.

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: This is usually a thin metal mesh that acts as a scaffold.

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: It's important to know that while the stent opens up an artery in your heart, it will

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: not help with plaque buildup that may be in other arteries in your body.

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: If you have a stent, this medication helps prevent clots from forming inside it.

segments.title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
segments.text_en: Your doctor will insert a flexible guide wire into the artery and advance a balloon-tipped

segments.title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
segments.text_en: catheter over this wire and into position at the side of the blockage.

segments.title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
segments.text_en: Rapid inflation and deflation of the small balloon pushes the vessel wall out,

segments.title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
segments.text_en: reestablishing blood flow through the artery.

segments.title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
segments.text_en: You may feel some chest pain while this is happening.

segments.title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
segments.text_en: The same procedure is repeated for each blockage being treated.

segments.title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
segments.text_en: Afterwards, your doctor will withdraw the deflated balloon and catheter from your arteries.

segments.title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
segments.text_en: Some patients will receive a coronary stent, which is a small tube made of wire mesh,

segments.title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
segments.text_en: designed to prop open the artery once it has been treated.

segments.title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
segments.text_en: A collapsed stent is placed over the deflated balloon as it's moved into position.

segments.title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
segments.text_en: When the balloon is inflated, the stent expands and locks into place.

segments.title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
segments.text_en: After the balloon and catheter are withdrawn, the stent remains behind to hold the artery open permanently.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: If you or someone you know has recently had a coronary artery bypass graft or cabbage,

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: this video will help you understand the steps of the procedure and how it restores blood flow to your heart.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: A coronary artery bypass graft or cabbage is performed to improve circulation to the heart muscle.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: In this procedure a healthy artery or vein from another part of the body is connected or grafted to the blocked coronary artery.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: The grafted artery or vein bypasses the blocked portion of the coronary artery, carrying oxygen-rich blood to the heart muscle.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: One or more coronary arteries may be bypassed during a single operation.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: Before the surgery, an intravenous line will be started and you may be given a medication to help you relax.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: Cabbage procedures are done under general anesthesia, which will put you to sleep for the duration of the operation.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: A breathing tube will be inserted through your mouth and into your throat to help you breathe.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: A catheter will be placed in your bladder to drain your urine.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: Coronary artery bypass surgery generally takes 3 to 6 hours.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: During a conventional bypass surgery, your surgeon begins by making an incision in the skin over your breastbone or sternum.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: He or she will then cut the sternum and move your ribcage in order to get to your heart.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: Throughout the procedure, your circulatory system may be connected to a cardiopulmonary bypass pump or heart lung machine.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: This machine may temporarily perform the functions of your heart and lungs during the surgery, allowing your heart to be stopped if desired while the surgeon shows the grafts into place.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: Different blood vessels may typically be used for the grafts.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: The internal thoracic arteries in the chest, also known as internal mammary arteries, or the saffinous veins in the legs.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: For the internal thoracic artery graft, your surgeon will leave the upper end attached to the subclavian artery and divert the lower end from your chest wall to your coronary artery just beyond the blockage.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will then sew the graft into place.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: For the saffinous vein graft, your surgeon will suture one end to the aorta and the other end to the narrowed artery just beyond the blockage.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: With the grafts securely in place, your surgeon may use electrical signals to restore the heart beat and attach a temporary pacemaker to the heart.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: Once your heart is again beating normally, if the surgeon used the heart lung machine, it would be disconnected.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will wire the breastbone back together and suture the skin incision closed.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: A temporary drainage tube will be placed through the skin beneath the incision.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: When the surgeon chooses to do the surgery without using the heart lung machine, the heart continues to beat.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: This may be referred to as off-pump bypass surgery or minimally invasive surgery.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: Instead, a mechanical device is used to steady the part of the heart where the grafting is being done.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: Surgeons perform minimally invasive bypass procedures using specially designed instruments inserted through small incisions or ports in the chest.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: After surgery, you will be taken to the intensive care unit.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: The activity of your heart will be carefully monitored.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: If necessary, pacing wires will be used to temporarily control your heart rate.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: The chest tube will remain in place to drain excess blood and air from the chest cavity.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: Once you can breathe on your own, your breathing tube will be removed and replaced with an oxygen mask.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: The bladder catheter will remain in place.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: As you recover from surgery over the next three to five days, all of these devices will be gradually removed.

segments.title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
segments.text_en: Upon discharge from your procedure, be sure to contact your doctor if you have any questions or experience any side effects from the medications you are taking.

segments.title: ANCE00178S102 How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages
segments.text_en: To begin the procedure, the doctor will numb the skin in your groin.

segments.file: ANCE00178S102_How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages_.mp4
segments.title: ANCE00178S102 How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages

segments.title: ANCE00178S102 How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages
segments.text_en: Next, a flexible guide wire will be passed through the needle into your artery.

segments.file: ANCE00178S102_How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages_.mp4
segments.title: ANCE00178S102 How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages
segments.text_en: Then, the needle will be withdrawn.

segments.file: ANCE00178S102_How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages_.mp4
segments.title: ANCE00178S102 How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages
segments.text_en: It will be exchanged for a small, flexible tube called a sheath.

segments.file: ANCE00178S102_How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages_.mp4
segments.title: ANCE00178S102 How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages
segments.text_en: This permits access into your artery.

segments.file: ANCE00178S102_How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages_.mp4
segments.title: ANCE00178S102 How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages
segments.text_en: Next, the guide wire will be advanced up to your heart.

segments.file: ANCE00178S102_How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages_.mp4
segments.title: ANCE00178S102 How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages
segments.text_en: A flexible tube called a catheter will be advanced over the wire to your coronary arteries.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: Coronary artery angiography is an x-ray test of the heart.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: This test can view blood vessels in your heart called coronary arteries.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: It can find out if a waxy substance called plaque has narrowed these vessels.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: And, it can show how well the valves and chambers of your heart are working.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: Coronary angiography is often done with a medical procedure called cardiac catheterization.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: Before the procedure, you will be placed on a table with an x-ray device called a florescope.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: Usually cardiac catheterization takes place through the radial artery in your wrist.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: This route normally has less bleeding and complications.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: Sometimes the femoral artery in your groin may be used instead.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: To begin, the doctor will numb the skin in your wrist.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: A needle will be inserted into your radial artery.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: A wire will be passed through this needle.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: The wire will be gently guided through your arteries to your heart.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: The needle will be taken out and replaced with a small flexible tube called a sheath.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: This permits access to your radial artery.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: Next, a soft flexible tube called a catheter will be slipped over the wire through the sheath.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: The catheter will be threaded up to your heart.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: Then, the florescope will be moved around your chest to take x-ray images of your heart.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: Your doctor will use the images to check on the catheter.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: You may feel pressure as the wire and sheath are put in, but you will not feel them inside your arteries.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: Once the catheter reaches your coronary arteries, the wire will be removed.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: The catheter tip will be placed just inside each artery to inject a special die.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: This die allows the florescope to take x-ray pictures of your arteries, called angiograms.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: Any blockages will be clearly identified as the arteries fill with die.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: You may feel flushed or slightly nauseated when the die is injected.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: At the end of the procedure, the catheter will be removed through your wrist.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: A pressure band will be used over the radial artery to prevent bleeding.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: Then, the sheath will be removed.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: The pressure band will remain on your wrist until bleeding has stopped.

segments.title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
segments.text_en: To find out more about coronary angiography, talk to your healthcare provider.

segments.title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
segments.text_en: An echocardiogram is a common test using sound waves to map out the shape and size of your heart,

segments.title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
segments.text_en: thus allowing your doctor to see how well your heart pumps blood and look for abnormalities of the heart valves and chambers.

segments.title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
segments.text_en: In a trans thoracic echocardiogram, your synographer will put a cool gel on your chest.

segments.title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
segments.text_en: Your synographer will move a transducer firmly through the gel, sending painless sound waves to your heart.

segments.title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
segments.text_en: The sound waves bounce off your heart and echo back to the transducer.

segments.title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
segments.text_en: The sound waves are converted to moving images of your heart muscles, chambers, and valves on a video screen.

segments.title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
segments.text_en: A Doppler echocardiogram records echoes from blood cells.

segments.title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
segments.text_en: The speed and direction of the blood flow indicate how well blood gets around inside your heart.

segments.title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
segments.text_en: A stress echocardiogram evaluates images of the heart before and after exercise or following medication that simulates exercise on the heart.

segments.title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
segments.text_en: Comparing your echo results before and after activity provides your doctor information about certain heart problems, such as coronary artery disease, that may only occur with exertion.

segments.title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
segments.text_en: A transisophageal echocardiogram sends sound waves through a transducer past gently down your throat while your under sedation.

segments.title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
segments.text_en: This test provides a closer, more detailed evaluation of your heart.

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: The heart is a muscular organ with four chambers.

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: Blood collects in the heart's two upper chambers or atria before filling the two lower

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: chambers or ventricles.

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: In the ventricles contract, blood in the right ventricle is pumped to the lungs and blood

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: in the left ventricle is pumped to the rest of the body.

segments.title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will perform an abdominal aortic aneurysm repair to prevent the rupture of a bulging

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: The Sinoatrial node or SA node is located in the wall of the right upper chamber of the

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: heart, called the right atrium.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will perform an aortic valve replacement if your aortic valve is not functioning properly.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: The heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood through the body.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: It consists of four chambers. The upper chambers are the right and left atria.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: The lower chambers are the right and left ventricles.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: The right and left atria connect to their respective ventricles.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: The right ventricle connects to the pulmonary artery and the left ventricle to the aorta.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Valves are located at these connections to ensure that blood moves in only one direction.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: The parts of a valve that open and close are flaps of tissue called cusps.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: The aortic valve is located between the left ventricle and the aorta.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: When the heart contracts, the aortic valve opens and blood is pumped into the aorta.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: When the heart relaxes, it closes to prevent blood from leaking back into the left ventricle.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: A normal aortic valve has three smooth cusps that open widely and close tightly.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Your aortic valve may be leaky or narrowed, causing your heart to work harder and disrupt your blood flow.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: A leaky valve does not close all the way, causing blood to leak back into the ventricle between each heartbeat.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: This condition is called aortic regurgitation.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: A narrowed aortic valve does not open all the way, which restricts the amount of blood passing out of the ventricle into the aorta.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: This condition is called aortic stenosis.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: You may have been born with an abnormal aortic valve.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: For example, your aortic valve may only have two cusps instead of three, so it may not open and close properly.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Your aortic valve may have become damaged during your lifetime, a condition called acquired aortic valve disease.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Causes include rheumatic fever, which can be a complication of untreated strep throat.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Andocarditis, which is a bacterial infection of the valve, and sclerosis or hardening of the aortic leaflets.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: After a period of time, a faulty aortic valve can cause the heart muscles to become enlarged and weakened.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: An aortic valve replacement is performed to restore the valve to normal function.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Once the anesthesia takes effect, your surgeon will begin by making an incision in the chest.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: The traditional incision is made in the middle of the chest, overlying the breastbone or sternum.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: The surgeon then separates the sternum to expose the pericardium, which is a protective membrane encircling the heart.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will then open the pericardium to expose the heart.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Once the heart lung machine has taken over, your heart will be temporarily stopped and the blood inside removed.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: This keeps the aortic valve still and allows your surgeon to see it.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will open the aorta to expose the aortic valve, make an incision around the edge of the damaged valve, and remove it.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will then carefully sew the replacement valve into place and close the aorta with stitches.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: At this point, blood will again be allowed to flow into your heart to check the function of the new valve.

segments.title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
segments.text_en: and atrial septal defect, which is when a hole between the right and left upper chambers

segments.title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
segments.text_en: of the heart, fails to close during development.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: Your heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood containing the oxygen and nutrients your

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: body needs.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: The pumping sections of the heart are the right atrium, right ventricle, left atrium,

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: and left ventricle.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: Your heart is an extraordinary machine.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: During your lifetime, your heart's muscular walls pump blood into blood vessels, branching

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: throughout your body.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: Your heart has four chambers.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: Two upper chambers, called the left and right atria, and two lower chambers, called

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: the left and right ventricles, contract in a steady rhythm known as your heart beat.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: During a normal heart beat, blood from your tissues and lungs flows into your atria, then

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: into your ventricles.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: Inside your heart, called the inter atrial and interventricular septa, help keep the

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: blood on left and right sides from mixing.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: Two valves sit like doors between your atria and ventricles to prevent blood from flowing

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: backward into your atria.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: The tricuspid valve opens into your right ventricle, and the mitral valve opens into your

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: left ventricle.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: Strong, thin tissues, called corda tendonae, hold your valves in place during the forceful

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: contractions of your ventricles.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: Blood leaving the ventricles passes through another set of valves, the pulmonary valve

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: between your right ventricle and pulmonary trunk, and the aortic valve connecting your

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: left ventricle and aorta.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: In order to pump blood more efficiently, your heart muscle, called myocardium, is arranged

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: in a unique pattern.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: Three layers of myocardium wrap around the lower part of your heart.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: They twist and tighten in different directions to push blood through your heart.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: Your heart is divided into left and right halves, which work together like a dual pump.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: At the top of your aorta, arteries branch off to carry blood to your head and arms.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: Arteries branching from the middle and lower parts of your aorta, supply blood to the

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: rest of your body.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: In hypoplastic left heart syndrome, the left side of a newborn baby's heart hasn't

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: developed completely.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: The heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood containing the oxygen and nutrients that

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: The pumping sections of the heart are the right atrium, right ventricle, left atrium,

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: and left ventricle.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: In a normal heart, oxygen poor blood flows from the body through large veins, called the

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: superior vena cava and inferior vena cava, into the right atrium.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: In hypoplastic left heart syndrome, a newborn baby's heart has severe birth defects that

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: prevent blood from flowing normally to the rest of the body.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: If the body can't get oxygen and nutrients, it can't survive.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: The left ventricle, which is the main pump and the aorta, which is the main pipeline,

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: are smaller than normal, so the baby's body does not receive enough blood.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: If the foreamen ovale and ductus arteriosus are allowed to close as they would normally,

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: blood flow to the body will decrease even more.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: Resulting in a life-threatening situation

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: Symptoms of hypoplastic left heart syndrome include gray or bluish skin color and rapid

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: breathing as the baby tries to get more oxygen to the body.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: Without surgical intervention, this condition is fatal.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: Surgeons will perform three separate operations to treat hypoplastic left heart syndrome.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: The goal of these operations is to improve the flow of oxygen-rich blood to the baby's body.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: The Stage 1 operation, called the Norwood procedure, is performed during the first week

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: after birth.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: During the operation, the surgeon will make three main changes.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: First, the pulmonary artery will be separated from the right and left pulmonary arteries

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: and connected to the aorta.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: A small piece of tubing, called a graft, will be inserted to connect a branch of the aorta

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: and the right pulmonary artery.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: Finally, the surgeon will remove the wall between the right and left atria.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: These changes increase the flow of oxygen-rich blood from the lungs through both atria

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: The Stage 2 operation, called the Glenn procedure, is performed about six months later when

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: the baby is stronger.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: The surgeon will remove the graft connecting the branch of the aorta and the right pulmonary

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: artery.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: To replace the graft's function, the surgeon will disconnect the superior vena-cava from

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: the heart and reconnect it to the right pulmonary artery.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: This connection allows oxygen-poor blood from the head and arms to go directly to the lungs

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: The Stage 3 operation, called the Fontan procedure, is performed when the baby is 18 months to

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: three years old.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: The surgeon will disconnect the inferior vena-cava from the heart and reconnect it to the

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: right pulmonary artery with graft tubing.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: This connection allows oxygen-poor blood from the lower body to go directly to the lungs

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: The Fontan procedure completes the surgical repair of the heart by achieving these goals.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: The discovery of oxygen-rich blood to the body through the creation of a new and expanded

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: aorta, creation of a new path for oxygen-poor blood to reach the lungs, and prevention of the

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: These three operations relieve the life-threatening condition caused by hypoplastic left heart syndrome

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: but do not restore normal circulation through the heart.

segments.text_en: body needs.

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: The heart has four muscular pumping chambers, the right atrium, the left atrium, the right

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: ventricle, and the left ventricle.

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: The left ventricle pumps blood out through a large artery called the aorta to supply

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: the body with the oxygen and nutrients it needs.

segments.title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
segments.text_en: In your heart there are two lower chambers called ventricles which contract with each heart beat.

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: Your heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood containing the oxygen and nutrients

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: The main pumping chamber of your heart is the left ventricle.

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: In your left ventricle contract, it sends oxygen-rich blood to your body through a large

segments.title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
segments.text_en: artery called the aorta.

segments.title: ANH19225 Understanding Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: Petrology of fallow is a birth defect that changes normal blood flow through a baby's

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: The treatments for tetralogyofollow are surgical procedures to help fix the following four

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: heart problems.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: A ventricular septal defect, or VSD, is a hole in the wall between the two ventricles.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: An overriding aorta happens when the aorta is located directly over the ventricular

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: septal defect.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: This means the aorta is connected to both the left and right ventricles instead of its

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: normal attachment to only the left ventricle.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: In pulmonary stenosis, the main pulmonary artery is narrowed, and the pulmonary valve

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: doesn't open all the way.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: And right ventricular hypertrophy means the wall of the right ventricle is thicker than

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: normal.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: Tetralogyofollow is repaired with open heart surgery, either soon after birth or in

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: infancy.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: Pricle procedures may include temporary and complete repairs.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: Your child may not be ready for a complete repair right away.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: In this case, a temporary repair, such as a shunt or a stent, can be placed until a complete

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: repair can be done.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: Both repairs increase blood flow to the lungs and improve oxygen levels in the blood delivered

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: to the body.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: During a shunt procedure, the surgeon will open the chest wall to place a tube called a

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: shunt between a branch of the aorta and the right pulmonary artery.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: During a stent procedure, a tube called a catheter will be inserted through a small incision

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: into a blood vessel in the leg.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: The cardiologist will use the catheter to place a device called a stent inside the narrowed

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: area of the pulmonary artery.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: A complete repair is done when your child is strong enough for the procedure.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: During the procedure, the pulmonary stenosis defect may be repaired in the following ways.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: If the main pulmonary artery is too small, it will be widened with a patch.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: If the pulmonary valve is too small, it may be widened with a patch or replaced.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: If extra heart muscle is blocking blood flow below the pulmonary valve, the surgeon will

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: remove the muscle tissue.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: These repairs will improve the flow of oxygen poor blood from the right ventricle to the lungs.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: To fix the ventricular septal defect, the surgeon will cover the hole with a patch.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: The patch will prevent the mixing of oxygen poor blood with oxygen rich blood.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: As a result, only oxygen rich blood will flow out of the aorta to the body from the left

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: ventricle.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: Over time, fixing the pulmonary stenosis and closing the ventricular septal defect will

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: also fix the right ventricular hypertrophy.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: Since the right ventricle won't have to work as hard to pump blood, its wall will return

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: to normal thickness.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: If your child still has a shunt from the temporary repair, it will either be removed or

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: clipped shut at the end of this procedure.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: Similarly, if your child had a stent placed, it will also be removed.

segments.title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
segments.text_en: To find out more about treatment for tetralogy of follow, talk to your health care provider.

segments.title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
segments.text_en: As you get older, your aortic valve may develop deposits of calcium that cause your leaflets

segments.file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
segments.title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
