segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: If you have a severe problem with the mitral valve in your heart, your doctor may recommend

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: a robotic mitral valve replacement if it can't be repaired.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Your mitral valve is one of the four valves in your heart.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: It lies between your left atrium or upper chamber and left ventricle or lower chamber.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Oxygen-rich blood coming from your lungs goes into the left atrium.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: When your left atrium contracts, blood flows through your open mitral valve and into your

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: left ventricle.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: When your left ventricle contracts, your mitral valve closes to prevent blood from flowing

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: back into your left atrium.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Your mitral valve has two leaflets, an anterior or front leaflet, and a posterior or back

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: leaflet.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: String-like tissue, called tendinous cords, attach both leaflets to the inside of your

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: left ventricle.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: The tendinous cords help prevent blood from flowing back into your atrium.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: You may have one or both of the two main problems that can happen to your mitral valve.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: One problem is stenosis, in which the opening in your mitral valve is two narrow.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: The narrow opening reduces the amount of blood flowing through the valve from your left

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: atrium to your left ventricle.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Your mental valve stenosis may be caused by rheumatic fever or heart-birth defects.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: The other problem your valve may have is regurgitation, in which your mitral valve doesn't

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: close completely.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Retraction allows blood to leak back into your left atrium when your left ventricle contracts.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Mitral valve regurgitation may be caused by mitral valve prolapse, in which the valve leaflets

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: and cords become weak and loose.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Rheumatic heart disease, which may result from rheumatic fever, heart-birth defects, or

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: heart enlargement, called dilation, which may result from a heart attack.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Both stenosis and regurgitation strain your heart because it has to pump harder to move

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: oxygen-rich blood to your body.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Before your procedure, an intravenous line will be started.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: You may be given antibiotics through the IV to decrease your chance of infection.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: You will be given general anesthesia to make you unconscious and pain-free during the

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: procedure.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: A breathing tube will be inserted through your mouth and down your throat to help you

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: breathe during the procedure.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will typically make four or five small incisions on the right side of your

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: chest.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: An assistant will insert a special camera called an endoscope and other tools attached

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: to the robotic arms through the incisions.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Unlike standard laparoscopic instruments, these tools can rotate 360 degrees and have more

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: flexibility than the human wrist.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Seated at a special console, your surgeon will operate the robotic arms and the camera

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: with joystick-like controls and foot pedals.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: A computer will translate the exact movements of your surgeon's hands into precise movements

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: of the surgical tools.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will have a magnified three-dimensional stereoscopic view inside your body through

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: a high-definition vision system.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: After the robotic arms are in place, you will be placed on a heart-long machine.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: This machine will take over the functions of your heart and lungs while your heart is

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: stopped for your operation.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will begin the procedure on your heart by making an incision in your left

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: atrium to get to your mitral valve.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Next, all of your mitral valve's attachments to your heart will be cut and the valve will

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: be removed.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Your damaged mitral valve may be replaced with an artificial mechanical valve.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: A mechanical mitral valve will last a lifetime.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: However, they require you to take blood thinner medication for the rest of your life.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Alternatively, your mitral valve may be replaced with a valve made from human or animal tissue.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: A tissue mitral valve may not last as long as a mechanical valve.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: However, the natural tissue does not require blood thinner medication.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Once your new valve is in place, your surgeon will restart the beating of your heart and

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: remove the attachments to the heart-long machine.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: At the end of your procedure, your skin incisions will be closed with stitches, staples, surgical

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: glue, or skin closure strips.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: After your procedure, your breathing tube will be removed and you will be taken to the

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: intensive care unit or ICU for monitoring.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: You will be given pain medication as needed.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: You may continue to receive fluids and medications through your IV.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: You will be given blood thinner medication if you have a mechanical valve.

segments.title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: Most patients are released from the hospital about a week after the procedure.

segments.title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
segments.text_en: The largest artery in the body is the aorta, which is attached to the heart and receives blood directly from the left ventricle or main pumping chamber.

segments.title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
segments.text_en: The aorta is divided into four parts, the ascending aorta, aortic arch, thoracic aorta, and the abdominal aorta.

segments.title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
segments.text_en: The abdominal aorta is the last and largest section of the aorta.

segments.title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
segments.text_en: It supplies blood to the lower regions of your body, including the organs and the abdomen and pelvis.

segments.title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
segments.text_en: The abdominal aorta ends around naval level, where it splits into the two iliac arteries that carry blood to the legs.

segments.title: ANS00425 The Heart
segments.text_en: The heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood through the body.

segments.title: ANS00425 The Heart
segments.text_en: It consists of four chambers.

segments.title: ANS00425 The Heart
segments.text_en: The upper chambers are the right and left atria.

segments.title: ANS00425 The Heart
segments.text_en: The lower chambers are the right and left ventricles.

segments.title: ANS00425 The Heart
segments.text_en: The right and left atria connect to their respective ventricles, the right ventricle connects

segments.title: ANS00425 The Heart
segments.text_en: to the pulmonary artery, and the left ventricle to the aorta.

segments.title: ANS00425 The Heart
segments.text_en: Those are located at these connections to ensure that blood moves in only one direction.

segments.title: ANS00426 The Aortic Valve
segments.text_en: The aortic valve is located between the left ventricle and the aorta.

segments.title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: And aortic valve replacement is performed to restore the valve to normal function.

segments.title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: Once the anesthesia takes effect, your surgeon will begin by making an incision in the chest.

segments.title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: The traditional incision is made in the middle of the chest, overlying the breastbone or sternum.

segments.title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: The surgeon then separates the sternum to expose the pericardium, which is a protective

segments.title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: membrane encircling the heart.

segments.title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will then open the pericardium to expose the heart.

segments.title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: This keeps the aortic valve still and allows your surgeon to see it.

segments.title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will open the aorta to expose the aortic valve, make an incision around the

segments.title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: edge of the damaged valve, and remove it.

segments.title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will then carefully sew the replacement valve into place and close the aorta

segments.title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: with stitches.

segments.title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: At this point, blood will again be allowed to flow into your heart to check the function

segments.title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: of the new valve.

segments.title: ANS11477 Four Heart Chambers
segments.text_en: Your heart is a muscular organ made up of four chambers, the right and left atria, and the right and left ventricles.

segments.title: ANS11477 Four Heart Chambers
segments.text_en: During a normal heartbeat, the chambers of your heart contract and relax in a rhythmic pattern to deliver a consistent flow of blood throughout your body.

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: In the ventricles contract, blood in the right ventricle is pumped to the lungs and blood

segments.title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
segments.text_en: in the left ventricle is pumped to the rest of the body.

segments.title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
segments.text_en: Your blood carries oxygen and nutrients to all of your tissues and removes carbon dioxide

segments.title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
segments.text_en: and other waste products from them.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: The heart normally beats in a regular rhythm at 60 to 100 beats per minute.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: This steady and coordinated rhythm allows the heart to effectively pump blood.

segments.title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
segments.text_en: When the electrical signals reach the heart muscle, they cause it to contract and pump blood.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: The heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood through the body.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: When the heart contracts, the aortic valve opens and blood is pumped into the aorta.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: When the heart relaxes, it closes to prevent blood from leaking back into the left ventricle.

segments.title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
segments.text_en: At this point, blood will again be allowed to flow into your heart to check the function of the new valve.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: The aorta is the largest artery in your body. Your heart pumps fresh, oxygen-rich blood

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: through your aorta to deliver oxygen and nutrients to all your organs and tissues.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: Your heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood containing the oxygen and nutrients your

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: body needs.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: In your heart, oxygen poor blood flows from your body through large veins into your

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: right atrium.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: Next, your blood moves into your right ventricle, which contracts, sending blood out of the

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: heart to pick up oxygen from your lungs.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: Oxygen rich blood moves out of your lungs into your left atrium, then moves into your

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: left ventricle.

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: Finally, your left ventricle contracts, sending oxygen rich blood out of your heart to your

segments.title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
segments.text_en: body.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: Your heart is an extraordinary machine.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: During your lifetime, your heart's muscular walls pump blood into blood vessels, branching

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: throughout your body.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: During a normal heart beat, blood from your tissues and lungs flows into your atria, then

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: into your ventricles.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: They twist and tighten in different directions to push blood through your heart.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: Your heart is divided into left and right halves, which work together like a dual pump.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: On the right side of your heart, deoxygenated blood from your body's tissues flows through

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: large veins, called the superior and inferior vena cava, into your right atrium.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: Next, the blood moves into your right ventricle, which contracts and sends blood out of your

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: heart to your lungs to gather oxygen and get rid of carbon dioxide.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: On the left side of your heart, oxygen-rich blood from your lungs flows through your

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: pulmonary veins into your left atrium.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: The blood then moves into your left ventricle, which contracts and sends blood out of your

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: heart through the aorta to feed your cells and tissues.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: At the top of your aorta, arteries branch off to carry blood to your head and arms.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: Arteries branching from the middle and lower parts of your aorta, supply blood to the

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: rest of your body.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: Your heart beats an average of 60 to 100 beats per minute.

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: In that one minute, your heart pumps about 5 quarts of blood through your arteries,

segments.title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
segments.text_en: delivering a steady stream of oxygen and nutrients all over your body.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: The heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood containing the oxygen and nutrients that

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: the body needs.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: In a normal heart, oxygen poor blood flows from the body through large veins, called the

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: superior vena cava and inferior vena cava, into the right atrium.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: Next, blood moves into the right ventricle, which contracts.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: Sending blood to the lungs through the pulmonary artery.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: Blood picks up oxygen in the lungs and returns through the pulmonary veins to the left atrium,

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: then moves into the left ventricle.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: Finally, the left ventricle contracts.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: Sending oxygen-rich blood to the body through the aorta.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: Forcing the baby's blood to travel to the lungs to pick up oxygen.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: These changes increase the flow of oxygen-rich blood from the lungs through both atria

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: into the right ventricle, increase the blood flow to the body through the new aorta,

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: and increase the blood flow to the lungs through the graft.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: This connection allows oxygen-poor blood from the head and arms to go directly to the lungs

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: to pick up oxygen.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: This connection allows oxygen-poor blood from the lower body to go directly to the lungs

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: to pick up oxygen.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: The discovery of oxygen-rich blood to the body through the creation of a new and expanded

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: aorta, creation of a new path for oxygen-poor blood to reach the lungs, and prevention of the

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: mixing of oxygen-rich blood and oxygen-poor blood.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: but do not restore normal circulation through the heart.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: Your heart pumps blood to your brain through blood vessels in your neck.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: These blood vessels, called arteries, branch into even smaller arteries that bring blood

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: to your brain cells.

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: In your heart, two chambers, called ventricles, contract with each heartbeat to push blood

segments.title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
segments.text_en: to your lungs and through your arteries to your body.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: Depending on activity level, the heart beats about 60 to 100 times per minute.

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: A normal heart rate and rhythm ensures the delivery of oxygen-rich blood to all of the

segments.title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
segments.text_en: body's organs, such as the brain and lungs.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: Depending on activity level, the heart beats about 60 to 100 times per minute.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: It may be higher during exercise or lower at rest.

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: A normal heart rate and rhythm ensures the delivery of oxygen-rich blood to all of the

segments.title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
segments.text_en: body's organs, such as the brain and lungs.

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: The heart has four muscular pumping chambers, the right atrium, the left atrium, the right

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: ventricle, and the left ventricle.

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: The left ventricle pumps blood out through a large artery called the aorta to supply

segments.title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
segments.text_en: the body with the oxygen and nutrients it needs.

segments.title: ANS11477 Four Heart Chambers
segments.text_en: During a normal heartbeat, the chambers of your heart contract and relax in a rhythmic pattern to deliver a consistent flow of blood throughout your body.

segments.title: ANS11477 Four Heart Chambers

segments.title: ANS11477 Four Heart Chambers

segments.title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will perform an abdominal aortic aneurysm repair to prevent the rupture of a bulging

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: Your aorta is divided into two main sections, the thoracic aorta and the abdominal aorta.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: The thoracic aorta has four parts, the aortic root, ascending aorta, aortic arch, and descending aorta.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: Over time, degenerative disease may cause the media layer to break down, weakening the wall of your aorta.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: Blood pumping against the weakened area may cause it to bulge outward like a balloon.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: When this condition occurs in your chest, it is called a thoracic aortic aneurysm.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: These aneurysms may occur in one of several places, including the aortic root, the ascending aorta, or the descending aorta.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: Most thoracic aneurysms have no symptoms, however, when they reach larger sizes, various symptoms may appear, depending on the severity and location of the aneurysm.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: Symptoms of stable thoracic aneurysms may include shortness of breath, chest, back, or abdominal pain, difficulty swallowing, and horse-ness.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: If the aneurysm ruptures or dissects, more dramatic symptoms will appear, including a ripping sensation in the chest, severe pain in the back between the shoulder blades, dizziness, and difficulty walking and speaking.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: If you have these symptoms, you should seek immediate medical attention, as this condition is life-threatening.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: Blood pumping against weakened aortic walls can lead to another life-threatening condition, called aortic dissection, which may or may not occur within an aneurysm.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: Most commonly, aortic dissection begins with damage to the intima layer.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: Blood moves through the break in the intima, separating it from the media, causing bleeding inside the wall of your aorta.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: As a result, a flap called a septum forms between the true aortic channel and the false channel.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: From here, the blood may break through the outer layer of the aortic wall, causing an immediate life-threatening condition, or it may re-enter the aorta through another tear in the intima.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: Over time, a blood clot may form in the false channel.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: An aortic dissection may be classified, according to its location and duration of symptoms.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: In the Stanford system, if you have an aortic dissection in your ascending aorta, it is called a type A dissection.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: All other dissections are called type B.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: The most common symptom of the aortic dissection is sharp or stabbing chest pain, usually sudden and severe.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: If you have a type A dissection, you're most likely to feel pain in your chest.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: If you have a type B dissection, you're more likely to feel pain in your back than your chest.

segments.title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
segments.text_en: If you have these symptoms, you should seek immediate medical attention, as this condition can be life-threatening.

segments.title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will perform an abdominal aortic aneurysm repair to prevent the rupture of a

segments.title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
segments.text_en: bulging area of your aorta.

segments.title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
segments.text_en: Open abdominal aortic aneurysm repair.

segments.title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will begin by making an incision from the breastbone to below the navel.

segments.title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
segments.text_en: He or she will then locate the aneurysm and clamp the aorta just above and below it.

segments.title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will then open the aneurysm and sew an artificial graft the same size and

segments.title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
segments.text_en: shape as a healthy aorta into place.

segments.title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will stitch the wall of the aneurysm closed over the newly placed graft.

segments.title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
segments.text_en: The graft will reinforce the walls of the aorta.

segments.title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will close the abdominal incision with stitches.

segments.title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
segments.text_en: The total length of this procedure is usually 4 to 6 hours.

segments.text_en: This condition is called deep vein thrombosis or DVT.

segments.title: ANS00433 DVT (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: Common symptoms of DVT include swelling of the leg or a vein in the leg, pain or tenderness

segments.title: ANS00433 DVT (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: in the area, increased warmth in the skin, and red or discolored skin.

segments.title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
segments.text_en: In some DVT cases, a blood clot may detach from a deep vein and join the bloodstream.

segments.title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
segments.text_en: The clot travels from the deep veins of the leg through the veins of the abdomen,

segments.title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
segments.text_en: into the heart, and finally lodges in the pulmonary artery or its branches, blocking blood flow

segments.title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
segments.text_en: to the lungs.

segments.title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
segments.text_en: This condition, called pulmonary embolism, is very serious and may permanently damage

segments.title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
segments.text_en: a portion of the lung.

segments.title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
segments.text_en: If a clot is large or if there are many clots, pulmonary embolism may also cause death.

segments.title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
segments.text_en: If you experience symptoms of PE, please tell your doctor or nurse immediately.

segments.title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
segments.text_en: These symptoms may include, shortness of breath, a sudden sharp pain in the chest, a feeling

segments.title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
segments.text_en: of lightheadedness or dizziness, rapid or irregular heartbeat, or coughing that produces

segments.title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
segments.text_en: blood.

segments.text_en: If you have hyperlipedemia, commonly known as high cholesterol, you have high levels of

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: One place triglycerides come from is your liver.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: So how do triglycerides get to the cells that need them?

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: Glycerides in food are digested and eventually reach your liver.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: In the liver, both the triglycerides from your food and those made in your liver are

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: packaged together with proteins.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: Each resulting molecule is called a lipoprotein.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: The lipoproteins travel throughout the body in your bloodstream.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: They deliver triglycerides to your cells which use them for energy.

segments.title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
segments.text_en: to get a blood test to measure your cholesterol levels.

segments.title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
segments.text_en: The National Institutes of Health cholesterol treatment guidelines recommend you have a blood

segments.title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
segments.text_en: test every five years if you are twenty years or older.

segments.title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
segments.text_en: The test, called a fasting lipoprotein profile, measures your levels of total cholesterol

segments.title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
segments.text_en: LDL, also known as bad cholesterol HDL, also known as good cholesterol

segments.title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
segments.text_en: and a type of fat in your blood called triglycerides.

segments.title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
segments.text_en: During this test, a blood sample will be taken after you have not eaten for nine to twelve

segments.title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
segments.text_en: hours.

segments.title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
segments.text_en: Helping is important to ensure accurate results for this test.

segments.title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
segments.text_en: The total cholesterol goal should be less than 200mg per deciliter.

segments.title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
segments.text_en: For most people, an ideal HDL or good cholesterol should be 60 or higher.

segments.title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
segments.text_en: An ideal LDL or bad cholesterol should be less than 100.

segments.title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
segments.text_en: If you have high blood cholesterol levels, you have a greater risk of heart disease.

segments.title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
segments.text_en: If you already have heart disease and multiple or severe risk factors, your doctor may lower

segments.title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
segments.text_en: your LDL goal to less than 70.

segments.title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
segments.text_en: If you have hyperlipedemia, commonly known as high cholesterol, you have high levels of

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: You or someone you know may have been diagnosed with high triglycerides.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: This video will show you what they are and how their levels are measured.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: High triglycerides can have harmful effects on your body.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: Triglycerides are a type of fat or lipid found in your blood.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: Your body uses triglycerides for energy.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: One place triglycerides come from is your liver.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: Another is from the food you eat.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: Foods that may raise your triglycerides include fatty foods such as hot dogs and tropical oils,

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: refined carbohydrates such as french fries, foods high in simple sugars such as donuts,

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: and alcohol.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: So how do triglycerides get to the cells that need them?

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: Glycerides in food are digested and eventually reach your liver.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: In the liver, both the triglycerides from your food and those made in your liver are

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: packaged together with proteins.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: Each resulting molecule is called a lipoprotein.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: The lipoproteins travel throughout the body in your bloodstream.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: They deliver triglycerides to your cells which use them for energy.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: Or if you have more than you need, fat cells store the triglycerides as body fat.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: If you are 20 years of age or older, the National Institutes of Health recommends you have

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: a blood test every 5 years.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: The test is called a fasting lipoprotein profile.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: During this test, a blood sample will be taken from your arm after you have not eaten

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: for 9-12 hours.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: This blood test can reveal whether your triglycerides fall into a healthy range.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: For most people, fasting triglycerides should be less than 150 mg per desoliter.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: 500 or higher puts you into the very high range.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: This means there is too much fat in your blood.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: Very high triglycerides can increase your risk of pancreatitis and other cardiovascular

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: events like heart disease.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: Ask your doctor what your triglyceride level should be.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: In many cases, lifestyle changes can help maintain healthy triglyceride levels.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: These changes include eating a diet low in sweets, refined carbohydrates and alcohol,

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: eating regular exercise, managing your weight and not smoking.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: Medications can also help treat impaired triglyceride levels.

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: If your doctor prescribed medication to treat high triglycerides, it is important to take

segments.title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
segments.text_en: it as directed and report any side effects you may have.

segments.text_en: If you or someone you know has been diagnosed with high cholesterol, it's important to keep

segments.title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
segments.text_en: and a type of fat in your blood called triglycerides.

segments.title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
segments.text_en: And fasting triglycerides should be less than 150.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: Your heart pumps blood to your brain through blood vessels in your neck.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: These blood vessels, called arteries, branch into even smaller arteries that bring blood

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: to your brain cells.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: Some of these small arteries join each other to form a loop known as the circle of willis.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: The walls of the arteries contain special muscle and elastic layers that help them adapt

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: to the blood pulsing through them.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: Sometimes diseases, injuries, or birth defects can cause a weak spot in the wall of the arteries

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: in the circle of willis.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: These weak spots usually form where arteries branch.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: Over time, the pressure of blood flowing through the artery stresses the weak spot further.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: The constant blood pressure can cause the weak spot to balloon out and form a bulge called

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: a cerebral aneurysm.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: This cerebral aneurysm is the most common type called a sacular or berry aneurysm.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: Over time, the aneurysm may grow and push on nearby brain tissue or nerves.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: Blood may also leak from the aneurysm.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: If its wall gets too thin, the aneurysm can burst or rupture and spill high pressure

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: blood into the space around your brain.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: The blood presses on your brain tissue and can cause injury to your brain cells.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: Chemicals in the escaped blood can irritate other brain arteries and cause them to narrow.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: As a result, your brain can swell, leading to serious brain injury.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: Common symptoms of a ruptured cerebral aneurysm include sudden severe headache, sometimes

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: called a thunder clap headache, problems with your vision such as blurred or double vision,

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: nausea and vomiting, a stiff or painful neck, or confusion.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: An unruptured cerebral aneurysm may not cause symptoms, but large unruptured aneurysms

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: may cause headache and pain near or behind one eye, double or blurred vision, a drooping

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: eyelid and numbness or weakness on one side of your face.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: A ruptured cerebral aneurysm is a medical emergency if you are having symptoms seek immediate

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: medical attention.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: If you have a ruptured cerebral aneurysm, your doctor may recommend surgical clipping

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: or endovascular coiling.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: In surgical clipping, your surgeon will make an opening in your skull, near your aneurysm,

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: and place a small metal clip onto the stem that connects the aneurysm to your artery.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: The clip blocks blood flow into your aneurysm.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: In endovascular coiling, your doctor will put a plastic tube called a catheter into a large

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: artery in your groin.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: Using a live X-ray view, your doctor will advance the catheter through your arteries until

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: it reaches the aneurysm.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: Through the catheter, your doctor will insert one or more flexible wires into your aneurysm.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: The wires will coil up and cause a blood clot to form inside your aneurysm, which will

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: block off the aneurysm from the rest of your artery.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: In a similar manner, if you have an unruptured cerebral aneurysm, your doctor may recommend

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: surgical clipping or endovascular coiling.

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: In addition, if your aneurysm is small and not causing pain or other symptoms, your doctor

segments.title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
segments.text_en: may observe your aneurysm for a period of time before performing either of these procedures.

segments.text_en: You will have a higher risk for other serious health problems such as a heart attack, stroke,

segments.file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
segments.title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
segments.text_en: and even death.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: This video will help you understand what a stroke is and the risk for having one.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: Normally, blood vessels called arteries carry blood to the brain.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: The brain needs a constant flow of blood to get the oxygen and nutrients it needs to work properly.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: During a stroke, also known as a brain attack, blood flow to part of the brain has stopped.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: The most common type of stroke is called ischemic stroke.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: It can happen in an artery where a fatty substance called plaque has made the artery narrow.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: In one type of ischemic stroke called a thrombotic stroke, the plaque can break apart and form a blood clot

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: that blocks the flow of blood to the brain.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: In another type of ischemic stroke called an embolic stroke, a blood clot forms in another area of the body and travels through the blood stream.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: If it reaches an artery near or in the brain, it can block the flow of blood.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: When an artery to part of the brain is blocked during a stroke, oxygen and nutrients in the blood can't get to those brain cells.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: The cells start to die within minutes.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: A less common type of stroke is called a hemorrhagic stroke.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: It occurs when a weakened blood vessel breaks open and leaks blood into the brain.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: This blocks blood flow to the brain and the leaking blood puts pressure on the brain tissue, which damages it.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: High blood pressure is the main risk factor for any type of stroke.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: Other risk factors include diabetes, certain types of heart disease, smoking, high cholesterol,

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: and having previous transient ischemic attacks.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: It's important to know these signs and symptoms of a stroke, a sudden numbness or weakness in your face, arms and legs, especially if it's on one side of your body.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: Sudden confusion and trouble speaking or being unable to understand others speaking.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: Sudden trouble seeing out of one or both eyes.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: Sudden dizziness, loss of balance or being unable to walk.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: And a sudden severe headache.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: Call 911 right away if you think you or someone else may be having a stroke.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: To find out more about your risk for having a stroke, talk to your healthcare provider.

segments.title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
segments.text_en: to the brain.

segments.title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
segments.text_en: When the blood flow is completely obstructed, as in a carotid artery blockage, a stroke

segments.title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
segments.text_en: can occur.

segments.text_en: If blood flow to the brain is blocked temporarily, a transient ischemic attack, also known as

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: a TIA, may occur.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: In a transient ischemic attack or TIA, a clot blocks the flow of blood to the brain.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: But it's different from the other types of stroke because the clot breaks up after a short time.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: Transient ischemic attacks usually don't cause any permanent damage.

segments.title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
segments.text_en: and having previous transient ischemic attacks.

segments.text_en: If blood flow to the brain is blocked temporarily, a transient ischemic attack, also known as a

segments.title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
segments.text_en: TIA, may occur.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: Your doctor may recommend you get a carotid and dardirectomy if your carotid arteries

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: are narrowed or blocked with plaque.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: In your neck are two major blood vessels called the carotid arteries, which carry oxygen

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: rich blood from your heart to your brain.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: You can feel the pulse of the carotid artery in the side of your neck.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: Three deposits called atherosclerotic plaques can build up inside the carotid artery.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: These plaques can narrow the passageway of the artery and interfere with blood flow

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: to the brain.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: This blockage or narrowing is called a stenosis.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: Before your procedure, an intravenous line will be started.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: A carotid and dardirectomy is usually done under general anesthesia, which will put you

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: to sleep for the duration of the procedure.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: In this case, a breathing tube will be inserted through your mouth and into your windpipe

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: to help you breathe during the operation.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: Sometimes a carotid and dardirectomy is done with local anesthesia.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: If local anesthesia is used, you will remain awake, but your neck will be numbed.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: You will probably also receive some sedation.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: On the side of your neck along the blocked artery, the surgeon will make an incision that

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: may run from just behind the ear to a point above the collarbone.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will find the carotid artery and temporarily clamp off blood flow through

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: it.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will open up the artery and may place a shunt or temporary bypass to keep blood

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: flowing to the brain.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: Your surgeon will then remove the plaque within the arterial walls.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: After the blockage has been removed, here she will sew the artery back together, usually

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: placing a patch to widen the artery slightly.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: The patch may be made of part of one of your veins or an artificial material such as

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: dachron.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: Finally, your surgeon will close the incision with sutures or staples and cover it with

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: bandages.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: A temporary drain is frequently left in the wound.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: After your operation, you will be taken to a recovery area where the staff will monitor

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: your vital signs and neurologic condition.

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: You will be given pain medication as needed and will gradually progress from a liquid to

segments.title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
segments.text_en: a solid diet.

segments.title: ANS00431 Carotid Arteries
segments.text_en: In your neck are two major blood vessels called the carotid arteries, which carry oxygen-rich

segments.title: ANS00431 Carotid Arteries
segments.text_en: blood from your heart to your brain.

segments.title: ANS00431 Carotid Arteries
segments.text_en: You can feel the pulse of the carotid artery in the side of your neck.

segments.title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
segments.text_en: In your neck are two major blood vessels called the carotid arteries, which carry oxygen-rich

segments.title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
segments.text_en: blood from your heart to your brain.

segments.title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
segments.text_en: You can feel the pulse of the carotid artery in the side of your neck.

segments.title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
segments.text_en: Fatty deposits called atherosclerotic plaques can build up inside the carotid artery.

segments.title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
segments.text_en: These plaques can narrow the passageway of the artery and interfere with blood flow

segments.title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
segments.text_en: to the brain.

segments.title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
segments.text_en: This blockage or narrowing is called a stenosis.

segments.title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
segments.text_en: If blood flow to the brain is blocked temporarily, a transient ischemic attack, also known as a

segments.title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
segments.text_en: TIA, may occur.

segments.title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
segments.text_en: When the blood flow is completely obstructed, as in a carotid artery blockage, a stroke

segments.title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
segments.text_en: can occur.

segments.text_en: Non-cardiac percutaneous transliminal angioplasty. A surgeon will perform a non-cardiac percutaneous

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Smoking increases your risk for peripheral vascular disease, in which atherosclerotic

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: plaques block the large arteries in your arms and legs.

segments.file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
segments.title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: Non-cardiac percutaneous transliminal angioplasty.

segments.file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
segments.title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: A surgeon will perform a non-cardiac percutaneous transliminal angioplasty, or NC-PTA, to restore blood flow

segments.file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
segments.title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: in an artery block by an atherosclerotic plaque.

segments.file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
segments.title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: To begin the procedure, you'll be placed on a table equipped with a fluoroscope.

segments.file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
segments.title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: A fluoroscope is capable of taking x-ray images of many parts of the body from multiple angles.

segments.file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
segments.title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: Your doctor will numb your skin around where the catheter is to be inserted.

segments.file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
segments.title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: Since the arteries of the leg are most easily accessed, the femoral artery in the groin serves as the insertion site in most cases.

segments.file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
segments.title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: Your doctor will initially gain access to the artery by inserting a hollow needle through which the balloon-tipped catheter is passed.

segments.file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
segments.title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: Watching on the fluoroscopy screen, your doctor will gently advance the catheter until it is positioned in the artery being treated and eject a special die.

segments.file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
segments.title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: This die allows the fluoroscope to take x-ray images called angiograms, which lets your doctor identify the exact location of the blockages.

segments.file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
segments.title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: Next, your doctor will move the catheter until the deflated balloon at its tip is positioned at the site of the stenosis.

segments.file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
segments.title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: Then, by rapidly inflating and deflating the balloon, your doctor will gently push the plaque and vessel wall out, partially re-establishing blood flow.

segments.file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
segments.title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
segments.text_en: This procedure may be repeated for any additional blockages.

segments.title: ANH22254 Understanding Sickle Cell Anemia
segments.text_en: Cyclecell anemia is an inherited blood disease that affects your red blood cells.

segments.title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
segments.text_en: Cycle cell anemia is an inherited blood disease that affects your red blood cells.

segments.title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
segments.text_en: In this disease, an abnormal protein molecule called hemoglobin S changes your disc-shaped

segments.title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
segments.text_en: flexible red blood cells into rigid, sickle-shaped cells.

segments.title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
segments.text_en: Cycle cells can block blood flow and can cause pain and tissue damage.

segments.title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
segments.text_en: And sickle cells don't last as long as normal red blood cells.

segments.title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
segments.text_en: As a result, your blood has less red blood cells than normal.

segments.title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
segments.text_en: This condition is called anemia.

segments.title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
segments.text_en: This replaces the sickle cells with healthy red blood cells.

segments.title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
segments.text_en: your bone marrow, where they will make normal red blood cells instead of sickle cells.

segments.text_en: Blood also helps fight infection and heal wounds.

segments.title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
segments.text_en: In leukemia, for example, the patient's bone marrow makes many abnormal white blood cells

segments.title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
segments.text_en: that can't do their job to fight infection.

segments.title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
segments.text_en: Over time, these abnormal cells crowd out the production of healthy white blood cells.

segments.title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
segments.text_en: In stem cell therapy for leukemia called hematopoetic stem cell transplantation, a doctor

segments.title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
segments.text_en: will take a sample of blood or bone marrow from the patient or a donor.

segments.title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
segments.text_en: In some cases, donor blood may come from a baby's umbilical cord after it's born.

segments.title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
segments.text_en: These tissue samples contain healthy hematopoetic or blood-forming stem cells.

segments.title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
segments.text_en: In addition, doctors will give the patient chemotherapy drugs or use radiation to kill

segments.title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
segments.text_en: the abnormal white blood cells and their stem cells.

segments.title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
segments.text_en: Once the abnormal cells are gone, the doctor will transplant the healthy hematopoetic stem

segments.title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
segments.text_en: cells from the tissue samples into the patient.

segments.title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
segments.text_en: These healthy stem cells will make new blood cells, including normal white blood cells,

segments.title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
segments.text_en: which will allow the body to fight off infections.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: For birth, the heart doesn't send blood to the lungs to get oxygen, since the baby

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: is not breathing yet.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: Instead, all the oxygen the baby needs passes from the mother's blood to the baby through

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: the placenta and a miracle cord.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: In addition, blood from the baby's heart bypasses the lungs in two ways.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: One is through a connection between the pulmonary artery and the aorta, called the ductus

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: arteriosus.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: The other is through an opening in the wall between the two atria, called the foreamen ovale.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: These extra openings allow oxygen-rich blood to bypass the baby's lungs and still circulate

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: through the body.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: Normally, within a day or two after birth, both the ductus arteriosus and the foreamen ovale

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: close.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: Forcing the baby's blood to travel to the lungs to pick up oxygen.

segments.title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
segments.text_en: If the foreamen ovale and ductus arteriosus are allowed to close as they would normally,

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Cigarette smoke contains at least 40 cancer-causing substances called carcinogens, including cyanide,

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: formaldehyde, benzene, and ammonia.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: If you smoke toxic chemicals, can damage the DNA in your healthy cells.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: As a result, your damaged cells create new unhealthy cells, which grow out of control

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: and may spread to other parts of your body.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: The most common cancer in the world is lung cancer, with over a million new cases diagnosed

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: every year.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Harmful chemicals in cigarettes can cause cancer in other parts of your body, such as, in the

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: blood and bone marrow, mouth, larynx, throat, esophagus, stomach, pancreas, kidney, bladder,

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: uterus, and cervix.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Over time, chronic bronchitis develops as your cilia stop working, your airways become

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: clogged with scars and mucus, and breathing becomes difficult.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Over time, so little oxygen can reach your blood that you may develop emphysema, a condition

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: in which you must gasp for every breath and wear an oxygen tube under your nose in order

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: to breathe.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: Chronic bronchitis and emphysema are collectively called chronic obstructive pulmonary disease

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: or COPD.

segments.title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
segments.text_en: COPD is a gradual loss of the ability to breathe for which there is no cure.
